summary: Sanity backend health check after YAML-only CI workflow fix in /app/.github/workflows/backend-tests.yml. Preview app is healthy, regression smoke band (30 tests) passes green, and GET /api/changelog returns valid JSON with expected keys. No app code was changed, no app-level regressions observed.
action_items: User must re-run the GitHub Actions backend-tests.yml workflow on GitHub to validate the actual CI fix — this can only be verified on GitHub infrastructure, not in this preview container.
critical_code_review_comments: YAML in /app/.github/workflows/backend-tests.yml is syntactically valid (yaml.safe_load passes). emergentintegrations==0.1.2 is installed BEFORE requirements.txt with --extra-index-url https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/ at line 67, as described. This is consistent with the recommended Emergent pattern to avoid litellm direct-URL spec conflicts.

engineering_log returns 25 entries; community_shipped is currently an empty list. Confirmed both keys present in /api/changelog JSON response (along with credit_cutoff). No regression observed vs. iteration_38.
test_credentials: none required (all three checks were public/health endpoints)
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_39 was a sanity pass after a CI-only YAML edit. Backend preview is healthy: GET /api/health=200 (mongo ok, scheduler running 11 jobs, storage ok, AV ok), regression smoke 30/30 passed in ~15s, GET /api/changelog=200 with engineering_log (25 items) + community_shipped + credit_cutoff keys. Actual CI validation must happen on GitHub by re-running backend-tests.yml.
rca of the issue: Not applicable — original bug was a CI-side pip install failure for emergentintegrations (private CDN, not PyPI) compounded by litellm direct-URL spec conflict. Fix was YAML-only: install emergentintegrations==0.1.2 with --extra-index-url BEFORE requirements.txt. Sanity confirms preview app was not impacted by the workflow edit.
